'''Liam Harrison''' (born 13 December 1983) is a former professional [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played representative level rugby league (RL) for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] and [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]], and at club level for [[Barrow Island ARLFC]] (in [[Barrow Island, Barrow-in-Furness]]) and [[Barrow Raiders]], with whom he spent his whole professional rugby league career, as a {{rlp|wg|ce|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}, and club level rugby union (RU) for [[North Walsham R.F.C.]] (in [[North Walsham]], [[Norfolk]]).<ref name="Bow out">{{cite web|title=The time is right for Harrison to bow out: Barrow Raiders boss Crarey|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/barrow-raiders/article/The-time-is-right-for-Harrison-to-bow-out-Barrow-Raiders-boss-Crarey-66386052-2501-4b87-83ce-3097f254454d-ds|publisher=North-West Evening Mail|accessdate=27 September 2016}}</ref>

==Background==
Liam Harrison was born in [[Barrow-in-Furness]], [[Cumbria]], England, he was a pupil at [[Chetwynde School]], and he is the older brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Ben Harrison (rugby league)|Ben Harrison]].

==Playing career==
Harrison joined his hometown club in 2005, having previously played for local amateur team Barrow Island.<ref>{{cite web|title=Island Crush Millom|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/news/millom/ISLAND-CRUSH-MILLOM-af8b824b-8286-4ef2-91b0-0cddf91c168e-ds|publisher=North West Evening Mail|accessdate=27 September 2016}}</ref> Harrison played with Barrow during several promotions and relegations between the [[Co-operative Championship|Championship]] and [[RFL League 1|League 1]], the highlight of his time being Barrow's victory in the [[2009 RFL Championship|2009 Championship]],<ref>{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=David|title=Barrow best of the rest after late rally|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/barrow-best-of-the-rest-after-late-rally-1797895.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220614/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/barrow-best-of-the-rest-after-late-rally-1797895.html |archive-date=14 June 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live|publisher=The Independent|accessdate=27 September 2016}}</ref> when Harrison was named in the league's team of the year.<ref>{{cite web|title=Co-Operative Championship All Star Teams Revealed|url=http://www.dewsburyrams.co.uk/news/article/38603/co-operative-championship-all-star-teams-unveiled|publisher=Dewsbury Rams|accessdate=27 September 2016}}</ref> He reached 300 appearances for the Raiders in 2016,<ref>{{cite web|title=Barrow Raiders ace Harrison reaches club milestone|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/barrow-raiders/article/Barrow-Raiders-ace-Harrison-reaches-club-milestone-39978f0f-975c-43cb-bb64-2d3655358201-ds|publisher=North West Evening Mail|accessdate=27 September 2016}}</ref> and retired at the end of the 2016 season.<ref name="Bow out" /> His [[testimonial]] match was held in Barrow between a Cumbrian XIII selected by Harrison, and the [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland national team]].<ref>{{cite web|title=NRL stars Coote and Linnett among Scotland's big names for Liam Harrison's testimonial|url=http://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/barrow-raiders/article/WATCH-NRL-stars-Coote-and-Linnett-among-Scotlands-big-names-for-Liam-Harrisons-testimonial-223818c2-a6c8-4b8b-a73f-02431b1f0b6c-ds|publisher=NorthWest Evening Mail|accessdate=14 November 2016}}</ref>

He is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international,<ref name="Bow out" /> and has represented [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]] against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]], and the [[United States national rugby league team|United States]] as well as playing in the [[County Championship (rugby union)|Rugby Union Counties Final]] at [[Twickenham Stadium|Twickenham]].
